name: Administration of Resources and Choices (Arc)
description_short: HUD-certified non-profit housing counselor organization in Arizona offering free homebuyer education, foreclosure prevention, and elder financial protection services.
description_long:
A.R.C. (Administration of Resources and Choices) is a non-profit organization based in Arizona, operating as a HUD-approved housing counseling agency. The organization was founded to help stabilize housing in the Tucson community and has expanded to offer comprehensive financial and housing services for residents of all ages, with particular focus on first-time homebuyers and seniors.

A.R.C. provides HUD-certified housing counseling services delivered by counselors with an average of 10+ years of experience. Their core offerings include homebuyer readiness programs that prepare consumers for mortgage qualification, down payment assistance programs (up to $40,000 for eligible first-time homebuyers), foreclosure prevention counseling for distressed homeowners, and reverse mortgage education for seniors. They also operate an Elder Protection Services program that addresses financial fraud, scams, and legal protections for seniors, as demonstrated by their work helping seniors regain control of finances after victimization.

A.R.C. distinguishes itself through its dual focus on housing stability and elder protection in a single organization. Their HUD certification ensures counseling meets federal standards, and their long-term counselor tenure suggests institutional knowledge and continuity. The organization operates on a donation-based model and actively recruits volunteers, indicating deep community integration rather than profit motive. Their emphasis on generational wealth building through homeownership and elder fraud prevention addresses underserved populations.

A.R.C. appears to be a legitimate, mission-driven non-profit with transparent operations. However, website content lacks specific details about service costs (free vs. fee-based), geographic service areas beyond Tucson, program eligibility requirements, or success metrics. Impact statistics shown on their homepage display as "0" across all categories, suggesting outdated or non-functional website functionality. Consumers should contact them directly to verify current service availability and specific program qualifications.
pros: HUD-certified housing counselors with average 10+ years of experience
Offers up to $40,000 in down payment assistance for qualifying first-time homebuyers
Provides specialized elder fraud and financial protection services
Free non-profit service model with no apparent profit motive or commission structure
Addresses foreclosure prevention and mortgage modification counseling for distressed homeowners
Reverse mortgage education and counseling services for seniors
Community-integrated with volunteer program and multiple donation pathways
cons: Website impact statistics display as '0' across all metrics, suggesting data tracking issues or outdated website functionality
Limited information about service area geographic boundaries; appears primarily focused on Tucson
No clear information about whether services are completely free or if nominal fees apply for certain programs
Sparse details on program eligibility requirements, income limits, or credit score thresholds
Down payment assistance program details and application process not fully explained on main pages reviewed
best_for: First-time homebuyers in Arizona seeking HUD-certified counseling and down payment assistance
Seniors concerned about financial fraud, reverse mortgages, or aging-in-place housing stability
Homeowners facing foreclosure or mortgage delinquency seeking loan modification counseling
diagnosis: A.R.C. is best for first-time homebuyers and seniors in Arizona seeking free, mission-driven HUD-certified housing counseling and elder protection services. Primary caveat: verify current service availability, geographic coverage, and program eligibility requirements by contacting them directly, as the website lacks comprehensive operational details and displays non-functional impact statistics.
services: First-time homebuyer education and mortgage preparation
Foreclosure prevention and loan modification counseling
Elder fraud and financial exploitation protection services
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 1625 N Alvernon Way Ste 101, Tucson, AZ 85712
meta_description: HUD-certified non-profit in Arizona offering free homebuyer education, foreclosure prevention, and elder financial protection services.
